arts.ttl: Chris Wood signs 2-year contract extension with Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest forward Chris Wood has signed a two-year contract extension to stay with the Premier League club through the 2026-27 season. “I’m very excited and very happy to have it completed and done, and I’m looking forward to the next couple of years, at least,” the 33-year-old Wood said in the team’s announcement Thursday evening.

arts.ttl: Chris Wood rewarded with new deal after firing Nottingham Forest into title race
arts.sum: The veteran striker has scored 14 Premier League goals this season in a remarkable career resurgence

arts.ttl: Morgan Gibbs-White has already given his honest opinion of Matheus Cunha amid Nottingham Forest pursuit
Chris Wood rewarded with new 2-year Forest deal
arts.sum: Chris Wood has signed a new deal at Nottingham Forest to extend his stay at the club until at least the end of the 2026-27 season.

arts.ttl: Chris Wood signs new two-year contract at Forest
arts.sum: New Zealand international Wood is the second Forest player to commit their future to the club this week, following Brazil defender Murillo, who agreed a deal earlier this week until 2029. He has backed the Club for years, even before promotion to the Premier League, which is fantastic, and the team is pushing in the right direction.

arts.ttl: Murillo signs new deal at Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest defender Murillo has signed a new contract that will keep him at the club until 2029. I’m thankful to everyone for the support – not just to me, but to my family as well,” Murillo told the club’s official website .
